originalTitle: Inside Kevin Warsh's selection process for the next Atlanta Fed president
summary: Choosing a new head for the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta is an opportunity for new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh to reshape the central bank.
content:
The search for a new president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta — now entering its seventh month — is being watched carefully for how new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh intends to reshape the interest rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee.

That process has shifted as Warsh has begun to put his stamp on the central bank . The Fed was considering candidates for Atlanta under then-Chair Jerome Powell , but the selection process was paused in part to allow Warsh to oversee the appointment, two people familiar with the hiring process told CNBC, speaking on condition of anonymity to discuss the ongoing search. Michael Faulkender , a former senior Treasury official under President Donald Trump , was subsequently considered for the position in Atlanta, the people said.

It isn't clear if Faulkender is still a candidate.

The Atlanta Fed declined to comment on Faulkender or who is under consideration.

"Our committee is conducting a thorough and deliberate search for the next president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta. We maintain our focus on selecting the best candidate to serve the Sixth District, while protecting the integrity of the process," Greg Haile, chair of the Atlanta Fed board of directors and presidential search committee, said in an emailed statement. "We will provide relevant updates about this important leadership role when appropriate."

The Fed and Faulkender declined to comment.

Warsh has promised "regime change" at the Fed, encompassing a rethink of rate-setting and balance sheet policy, communications, and what data the central bank uses to gauge the economy. But he inherits a Fed whose membership was shaped by Powell and the chairs before him. Warsh has few immediate opportunities beyond the Atlanta job to bring allies onto the FOMC.

He recently announced five task forces to study what he wants to change. Those will be staffed by outside experts. No current Fed governors or presidents will be included on those task forces.

Warsh comes in facing questions about how independent he'll be from Trump. But Warsh didn't acquiesce at his first FOMC meeting last week to Trump's demands for rapid rate cuts.

Instead Warsh defied many analysts' expectations with a hawkish news conference that prompted markets to raise their projections for interest rates.

Warsh could use the Atlanta appointment to further defy expectations and assert his independence from the administration.

The 12 regional Fed presidents are chosen through a back-and-forth process between the directors of the bank and the Board of Governors in Washington. That arrangement is the result of the Federal Reserve Act's effort to balance the power of the board in Washington and the influential New York Fed with additional regional representation.

Typically, the local bank's directors hire a search firm and come up with a list of candidates. After feedback from Washington, the directors then choose a president who is acceptable to the board and the chairman. The process can involve input from the board and from congressional representatives and advocacy groups.

Bank directors often have their own agendas. For example, the Kansas City Fed has elected a string of hawkish candidates going back decades. They have racked up the most dissenting votes on the FOMC against easing interest rates.

The Atlanta Fed's position has been open since February, when Raphael Bostic stepped down at the end of his term. He had announced his departure in November. He became the first African American to head a district bank in 2017 under pressure from Congress and outside interest groups for the Fed to diversify the ranks of its leadership.

Executive search firm Heidrick & Struggles is overseeing the current search. The firm didn't respond to a request for comment.

Warsh may eventually be able to reshape the committee. The Supreme Court will soon rule on whether Governor Lisa Cook can continue in her job, after Trump attempted to fire her last year. Powell holds a seat on the Board of Governors that expires in January 2028.

Three other regional bank presidents will be up for replacement in 2028: John Williams of New York, Mary Daly of San Francisco and Tom Barkin of Richmond.

There is already a quiet campaign to urge Warsh to line up a successor for Williams, CNBC has reported .

The president of the New York Fed has a permanent vote on the FOMC. The 11 other regional bank presidents, including Atlanta's, vote on a rotating basis. The Atlanta Fed president will vote on the FOMC in 2027. The Atlanta bank is currently run by interim President Cheryl Venable.

Powell expressed concern in his final news conference as chair about the possibility of political interference with the Fed's regional presidents. Were the regional presidents to be removed because of their policy views, "that would be the beginning of the end of, of the Fed's ability to, to make monetary policy independently," Powell said in April.

Warsh at his confirmation hearing in April said that although he had called for "regime change" at the Fed, he didn't mean that to include replacing the regional bank presidents.

Faulkender was deputy Treasury secretary from March to August 2025 and is now a finance professor at the University of Maryland. Faulkender, who served briefly acting commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service, resigned his Treasury position after Trump soured on him, the Wall Street Journal reported .

The ultimate choice for Atlanta will be closely scrutinized for any sign of loyalty to or independence from the Trump administration.

Two current regional Fed presidents, Austan Goolsbee of Chicago and Neel Kashkari of Minneapolis, previously held political appointments in different administrations.

Correction: Faulkender served briefly as acting commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service. An earlier version misstated the name of the agency.
chineseTitle: 凯文·沃什 遴选下一任亚特兰大联储主席的过程
chineseSummary: 为亚特兰大联储选择新行长是新任美联储主席凯文·沃什重塑央行的一个机会。
chineseContent:
亚特兰大联邦储备银行新任行长的寻找工作现已进入第七个月，人们正在密切关注新任美联储主席凯文·沃什打算如何重塑负责利率制定的联邦公开市场委员会。

随着沃什开始在央行留下自己的印记，这一过程发生了变化。两名熟悉招聘流程的人士告诉 CNBC，美联储正在考虑时任主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔 (Jerome Powell) 领导下的亚特兰大候选人，但遴选过程已暂停，部分原因是为了让沃什能够监督任命。知情人士称，唐纳德·特朗普总统领导下的前财政部高级官员迈克尔·福肯德随后被考虑担任亚特兰大的这一职位。

目前尚不清楚福肯德是否仍然是候选人。

亚特兰大联储拒绝对福肯德或正在考虑的人发表评论。

亚特兰大联储董事会兼行长遴选委员会主席格雷格·海尔在一份电子邮件声明中表示：“我们的委员会正在对亚特兰大联储下一任行长进行彻底、深思熟虑的搜寻。我们仍然致力于选择为第六区服务的最佳候选人，同时保护整个过程的完整性。” “我们将在适当的时候提供有关这一重要领导角色的相关更新。”

美联储和福肯德拒绝置评。

沃什承诺在美联储进行“制度变革”，包括重新考虑利率制定和资产负债表政策、沟通以及央行用来衡量经济的数据。但他继承的美联储的成员是由鲍威尔和他之前的主席决定的。除了亚特兰大的工作之外，沃什几乎没有立即将盟友带入联邦公开市场委员会的机会。

他最近宣布成立五个工作组来研究他想要改变的事情。这些人员将由外部专家组成。这些工作组中不会包括现任美联储理事或行长。

沃什面临着关于他将如何独立于特朗普的质疑。但沃什上周在首次联邦公开市场委员会会议上并没有默许特朗普快速降息的要求。

相反，沃什的鹰派新闻发布会出乎许多分析师的预期，促使市场上调利率预测。

沃什可以利用亚特兰大的任命来进一步打破预期，并宣称自己独立于政府。

12 位地区联储行长是通过银行董事和华盛顿理事会的来回程序选出的。这种安排是《联邦储备法》努力平衡华盛顿联储理事会和具有影响力的纽约联储的权力以及增加地区代表性的结果。

通常，当地银行的董事会聘请一家猎头公司并提出一份候选人名单。在得到华盛顿的反馈后，董事们会选出一位董事会和董事长都可以接受的总裁。该过程可能需要董事会、国会代表和倡导团体的意见。

银行董事通常有自己的议程。例如，堪萨斯城联储几十年前就选出了一系列鹰派候选人。他们在联邦公开市场委员会 (FOMC) 中获得了反对放松利率的最多反对票。

亚特兰大联储的职位自二月份拉斐尔·博斯蒂克(Raphael Bostic)任期结束后辞职以来一直处于空缺状态。他于11月宣布离职。 2017 年，在国会和外部利益团体要求美联储实现领导层多元化的压力下，他成为首位担任地区银行行长的非裔美国人。

猎头公司海德思哲 (Heidrick & Struggles) 正在监督当前的猎头工作。该公司没有回应置评请求。

沃什最终可能能够重塑该委员会。在特朗普去年试图解雇丽莎·库克州长后，最高法院很快将就州长丽莎·库克是否可以继续任职做出裁决。鲍威尔在理事会的席位将于 2028 年 1 月到期。

另外三名地区银行行长将于 2028 年进行更换：纽约的约翰·威廉姆斯 (John Williams)、旧金山的玛丽·戴利 (Mary Daly) 和里士满的汤姆·巴金 (Tom Barkin)。

据 CNBC 报道，已经有一场悄悄的活动敦促沃什为威廉姆斯挑选继任者。

纽约联储主席对联邦公开市场委员会拥有永久投票权。包括亚特兰大银行行长在内的其他 11 家地区银行行长实行轮流投票。亚特兰大联储主席将于 2027 年对 FOMC 进行投票。亚特兰大联储目前由临时行长谢丽尔·维纳布尔 (Cheryl Venable) 管理。

鲍威尔在担任主席的最后一次新闻发布会上表达了对美联储地区行长可能受到政治干预的担忧。鲍威尔四月份表示，如果地区主席因其政策观点而被罢免，“这将是美联储独立制定货币政策的能力终结的开始”。

沃什在四月份的确认听证会上表示，尽管他呼吁美联储进行“政权更迭”，但他的意思并不是包括更换地区银行行长。

福肯德于 2025 年 3 月至 8 月期间担任财政部副部长，现为马里兰大学金融学教授。据《华尔街日报》报道，福肯德曾短暂担任美国国税局代理局长，在特朗普对他不满后，他辞去了财政部职务。

亚特兰大的最终选择将受到密切关注，看是否有任何忠于特朗普政府或独立于特朗普政府的迹象。

两位现任地区联储主席，芝加哥的奥斯坦·古尔斯比和明尼阿波利斯的尼尔·卡什卡里，此前曾在不同的政府中担任过政治任命。

更正：福肯德曾短暂担任国税局代理局长。早期版本错误地表述了该机构的名称。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
